message:
feat(perf): extreme load resilience - SQLite hardening, async commit coalescer, write-behind queue

Addresses historical Python version bottlenecks (git lock contention, SQLite
connection exhaustion, synchronous I/O blocking tool responses):

- SQLite PRAGMAs: busy_timeout=120s, cache_size=64MB, mmap_size=512MB,
  wal_autocheckpoint=2000, temp_store=MEMORY, threads=4
- Pool sizing: 8 base + 12 overflow = 20 max connections, 60s timeout
- Async commit coalescer: background worker batches git commits with
  jittered exponential backoff, flush_async_commits() for tests/shutdown
- Write-behind queue integration: notification signals + archive writes
  processed via async WBQ with wbq_flush() for deterministic testing
- Conformance: fix agent name validation (adjective+noun format),
  add wbq_flush before signal assertions, fix TrackedTransaction usage
- DB cache module for in-memory query result caching

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-1bm.3.1","title":"Peer Addr Parity: integrate asupersync Http1Request.peer_addr","description":"Integrate `peer_addr` from `asupersync::Http1Request` into server logic.

This task is blocked on upstream asupersync work tracked as `br-2ei.6.3`.

Work:
- Once `Http1Request.peer_addr` is available, plumb it through the HTTP server request handling.
- Expose a helper to derive a stable `client_host` string from peer_addr (legacy-equivalent formatting).
- Ensure forwarded headers do not override peer_addr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Server has access to peer_addr for each request and uses it consistently for:
  - localhost bypass decisions
  - rate-limit identity (when JWT sub absent)
- `client_host` formatting matches legacy for:
  - IPv4
  - IPv6
  - IPv4-mapped IPv6
- Tests:
  - Unit tests validate formatting and “forwarded headers do not override peer_addr”.
  - Integration tests validate peer_addr is populated in the request path (using simulated Http1Request / test harness).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:05.220643258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:38:05.917468187Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:38:05.917450805Z","close_reason":"Integrated peer_addr from asupersync and wired into server logic","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:05.220643258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:08:07.059047396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.3.2","title":"Peer Addr Parity: localhost detection helper","description":"Implement localhost detection helper for peer_addr-based bypass logic.

Requirements:
- Handle IPv4, IPv6, and IPv4-mapped IPv6 localhost forms.
- Treat forwarded headers as disqualifying for localhost bypass (no spoofing).
- Use this helper consistently in bearer auth, RBAC decisions, and any other localhost-only paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correct detection:
  - `127.0.0.1`, `::1`, and IPv4-mapped `::ffff:127.0.0.1` are treated as localhost.
  - Non-localhost private IPs are *not* treated as localhost.
- Forwarded headers:
  - Presence of `X-Forwarded-For`/`Forwarded` (per legacy) disables localhost bypass.
- Tests:
  - Unit tests cover all address forms and forwarded-header cases.
  - Integration test verifies bypass behavior is controlled exclusively by peer_addr + forwarded headers (not client-provided host headers).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:09.028452900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:38:09.584845027Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:38:09.584827845Z","close_reason":"Added peer_addr localhost helper + forwarded header safeguards + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:09.028452900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.3.2","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:26.736664544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.3.3","title":"Peer Addr Parity: rate-limit identity plumbing","description":"Implement rate-limit identity plumbing for peer_addr-derived host.

Rule:
- Use JWT `sub` as the default rate-limit identity when present.
- Otherwise, use `client_host` derived from peer_addr.
- Identity string must match legacy formatting and be used consistently for rate limiter key creation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Identity derivation matches legacy precedence and formatting.
- Forwarded headers do not affect identity (peer_addr only).
- Tests:
  - Unit tests cover precedence (JWT sub vs peer_addr) and formatting.
  - Integration test asserts Redis key prefix + identity combine exactly as legacy expects.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:12.867654737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:38:13.146794229Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:38:13.146772948Z","close_reason":"Rate-limit identity now uses peer_addr; helper covers JWT sub precedence","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.3.3","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:12.867654737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.3.3","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:30.103821388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-1bm.4","title":"Stateless Streamable HTTP MCP Parity","description":"Objective:
Reproduce legacy Python stateless Streamable HTTP behavior for MCP requests, including header normalization, base path mount semantics, passthrough route, and localhost Authorization injection. Must be wire-compatible for existing MCP clients.

Legacy Reference Behavior:
- Per-request StreamableHTTPServerTransport, stateless=True
- Accept header forced to "application/json, text/event-stream"
- Content-Type added for POST if missing
- Base path mounted at both /base and /base/ (no redirect)
- Direct POST handler at base path that forwards to mounted app
- If localhost + allow_localhost_unauthenticated + bearer token set, inject Authorization if missing
- Responses passed through without extra wrapping

Implementation Considerations:
- Use fastmcp_rust transport primitives (no tokio)
- Preserve request/response JSON-RPC payloads exactly
- Avoid breaking SSE/streaming semantics

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
- Spec + fixtures: `br-1bm.4.1`
- Implementation: `br-1bm.4.2`..`br-1bm.4.5`
- Tests: `br-1bm.4.6` (uses shared harness `br-2ei.9.1`)

Dependencies:
- peer address parity (`br-1bm.3`) for localhost detection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wire behavior matches legacy Python for all fixture cases from `br-1bm.4.1`.
- Streaming responses remain incremental (not buffered) and have correct headers.
- `br-1bm.4.6` unit+integration+E2E suite passes with high-signal artifacts on failure.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:33:19.242331091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:39:44.206098931Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.4","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.4","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.4.1","title":"Streamable HTTP Parity: extract legacy behaviors + fixtures","description":"Purpose:
Extract exact legacy HTTP streamable behaviors and define fixture cases for parity.

Legacy Behavior (from http.py):
- Mount base:
  - mount_base = settings.http.path or "/api"
  - if not startswith("/"): prefix "/"
  - base_no_slash = mount_base.rstrip("/") or "/"
  - base_with_slash = base_no_slash if "/" else base_no_slash + "/"
  - fastapi_app.mount(base_no_slash, stateless_app)
  - fastapi_app.mount(base_with_slash, stateless_app)
- Direct POST handler at base_no_slash:
  - Forwards to stateless_app with path=base_with_slash
  - Collects response status/headers/body by intercepting send()
  - Parses body as JSON (empty => {})
- Header normalization:
  - Remove any existing Accept headers
  - Add Accept: "application/json, text/event-stream"
  - If POST and Content-Type missing, add Content-Type: "application/json"
- Localhost Authorization injection in passthrough:
  - If allow_localhost_unauthenticated and client_host in {127.0.0.1, ::1, localhost} and no Authorization header, inject Bearer token when configured

Fixture Cases (minimum):
1) Request to /api and /api/ both route to stateless app (no redirect)
2) POST /api with missing Accept -> Accept forced to "application/json, text/event-stream"
3) POST /api with existing Accept -> replaced (single header) with forced value
4) POST /api missing Content-Type -> set to application/json
5) POST /api with Content-Type present -> preserved
6) Direct POST handler returns exact status/body from stateless app
7) Localhost injection adds Authorization when missing (only if allow_localhost_unauthenticated + bearer token)
8) Forwarded headers present -> no localhost injection

Deliverable:
- Written fixture table (inputs -> expected headers + status + body)
- Use as baseline for br-1bm.4.* implementation/tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:51.705065959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:38:24.514040016Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:38:24.513965806Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.4.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:51.705065959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.4.2","title":"Streamable HTTP Parity: header normalization","description":"## Objective
Normalize headers for streamable HTTP requests to match legacy FastAPI adapter behavior.

## Scope
- Ensure `Accept: application/json, text/event-stream` is present (replace any existing Accept).
- Ensure `Content-Type: application/json` is present for POST when missing.
- Preserve other headers unmodified.

## Tests
- Unit tests for header normalization logic.
- Integration tests via HTTP client lacking Accept header (httpx default).

## Logging/Artifacts
- Capture request/response headers under `tests/artifacts/http/streamable/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:57.051364879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:11:49.109975991Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:39:57.051364879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:40:24.386470843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-2ei.13.2","title":"LLM Impl: tokio-free completion client (asupersync) + summarize_thread refinement","description":"Implement legacy LLM behavior in Rust.

Work:
- Config (core): parse missing LLM env vars to match spec `br-2ei.13.1`:
  - LLM_COST_LOGGING_ENABLED
  - LLM_CACHE_BACKEND / LLM_CACHE_REDIS_URL
- Provider env bridge:
  - apply mapping table (e.g., GEMINI_API_KEY -> GOOGLE_API_KEY) at startup / before first call
  - do not crash if .env missing
- LLM client abstraction:
  - introduce a small trait/interface so tests can inject a stub (no network)
  - implement real client(s) using asupersync HTTP:
    - at minimum: OpenAI-compatible chat completions + Google Gemini + Anthropic (as in legacy “best available” selection)
    - implement fallback-to-alt-model selection logic
  - normalize output into a stable struct `{content, model, provider, estimated_cost_usd?}`
- Cache (best-effort):
  - memory cache when enabled
  - optional redis cache if configured and reachable; if unreachable, fall back to memory/local
- Cost logging:
  - when enabled, emit a single structured log event per completion with model/provider/cost when known
  - never let logging break normal flow
- Tool integration:
  - `summarize_thread` supports llm_mode=true and llm_model override
  - implement refinement step exactly like spec (parse JSON response, validate shape, merge)
  - keep llm_mode path fully deterministic under stub client

Constraints:
- No tokio runtime.
- No network access required for unit tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `complete_system_user` works end-to-end with stub client and returns normalized output.
2. Real client implementation uses asupersync (no reqwest/tokio).
3. `summarize_thread` llm_mode semantics match spec and remain safe on invalid JSON (fallback behavior is spec-accurate).
4. All new config/env behavior is covered by unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:31:48.149361512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:08:02.749159340Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:08:02.749141376Z","close_reason":"Implemented in crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/llm.rs: env bridge, model selection, completion client (asupersync HttpClient), JSON parsing, merge logic, wired into summarize_thread. 20 tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.13.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:31:48.149361512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.13.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:32:18.795007885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.13.3","title":"LLM Tests: env bridge + completion normalization + summarize_thread llm_mode","description":"Add comprehensive tests for LLM parity.

Work:
- Unit tests (LLM module): mirror legacy tests:
  - provider env bridge mapping (GEMINI_API_KEY -> GOOGLE_API_KEY, etc)
  - completion helper returns normalized fields even when provider/router is missing/unavailable
  - cost-logging enabled path does not panic
  - cache enabled path does not panic; redis unavailable falls back
- Tool-level tests:
  - `summarize_thread` multi-thread mode with llm_mode=true uses stubbed completion response JSON and returns refined aggregate keys
  - invalid JSON refinement response triggers spec-accurate fallback (no crash)
- Integration tests:
  - start server with LLM enabled + stub client injected
  - exercise `summarize_thread` and `macro_prepare_thread` llm_mode paths
- E2E:
  - coordinate with `br-2ei.9.7` to run a stubbed llm_mode smoke, capture tool payloads + diffs

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test` includes LLM unit + tool + integration tests and passes.
- Tests are offline/deterministic (no network, no real API keys required).
- Failure output includes minimal diffs on JSON mismatches.
- E2E smoke validates llm_mode flows with artifact logs under tests/artifacts/llm/.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:31:57.009703582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:08:27.029825034Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:08:27.029806880Z","close_reason":"20 unit tests in llm.rs covering: JSON parsing (11 vectors), model selection, env bridge, merge logic (single + multi thread), prompt building. Tests match fixture vectors.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.13.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:31:57.009703582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.13.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:32:18.882197533Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"br-2ei.14","title":"TOON Output Format Parity: tools + resources (format=toon envelope)","description":"## Objective
Implement TOON output format parity for tools and resources, including envelope, stats, and fallback behavior.

## Scope (from spec)
- Env vars: `MCP_AGENT_MAIL_OUTPUT_FORMAT`, `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT`, `TOON_TRU_BIN`, `TOON_BIN`, `TOON_STATS`.
- Tool format application: if requested format=toon, encode JSON payload via external `tru` binary; on failure return JSON envelope with `toon_error`.
- Resource format application: `format=toon` query param; synchronous encoding.
- Envelope schema:
  - Success: `{format:"toon", data:<encoded>, meta:{requested, source, encoder, toon_stats{json_tokens, toon_tokens, saved_tokens, saved_percent}, toon_stats_raw}}`
  - Fallback: `{format:"json", data:<original>, meta:{requested, source, toon_error}}`
- Stats parsing from `tru` stderr when enabled, truncated to 2000 chars on parse failure.

## Tests
- Unit tests for envelope schema and fallback when encoder missing/fails.
- Integration tests invoking tool/resource with `format=toon` and verifying envelope.
- Conformance fixtures comparing to legacy envelope outputs.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store raw encoder stderr and envelope diffs under `tests/artifacts/toon/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:35:38.532820388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:10:43.192084657Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.14","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:35:38.532820388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-2ei.14","title":"TOON Output Format Parity: tools + resources (format=toon envelope)","description":"## Objective
Implement TOON output format parity for tools and resources, including envelope, stats, and fallback behavior.

## Scope (from spec)
- Env vars: `MCP_AGENT_MAIL_OUTPUT_FORMAT`, `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT`, `TOON_TRU_BIN`, `TOON_BIN`, `TOON_STATS`.
- Tool format application: if requested format=toon, encode JSON payload via external `tru` binary; on failure return JSON envelope with `toon_error`.
- Resource format application: `format=toon` query param; synchronous encoding.
- Envelope schema:
  - Success: `{format:"toon", data:<encoded>, meta:{requested, source, encoder, toon_stats{json_tokens, toon_tokens, saved_tokens, saved_percent}, toon_stats_raw}}`
  - Fallback: `{format:"json", data:<original>, meta:{requested, source, toon_error}}`
- Stats parsing from `tru` stderr when enabled, truncated to 2000 chars on parse failure.

## Tests
- Unit tests for envelope schema and fallback when encoder missing/fails.
- Integration tests invoking tool/resource with `format=toon` and verifying envelope.
- Conformance fixtures comparing to legacy envelope outputs.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store raw encoder stderr and envelope diffs under `tests/artifacts/toon/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:35:38.532820388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:14:48.060557256Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:14:48.060521288Z","close_reason":"All children closed (spec/impl/tests/e2e); TOON format parity implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.14","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:35:38.532820388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.14.1","title":"TOON Spec: extract encoder detection + envelope schema + stats + fallback","description":"Extract exact legacy Python toon-format behavior into a self-contained spec + vectors.

Legacy sources:
- `legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code/mcp_agent_mail/src/mcp_agent_mail/app.py`:
  - format handling for tools (format arg)
  - encoder detection (`_looks_like_toon_rust_encoder`)
  - encoder invocation (`_run_toon_encode`) and stderr parsing for stats
  - fallback behavior on encoder error
- Resources: where query param `format=toon` is handled and how it wraps blocks
- Config env vars:
  - TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT
  - TOON_STATS
  - TOON_TRU_BIN / TOON_BIN
- Tests:
  - `legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code/mcp_agent_mail/tests/test_toon_formatting.py`
  - `legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code/mcp_agent_mail/tests/e2e/test_toon_format_e2e.py`

Must capture verbatim:
- Envelope JSON shape for tools and resources:
  - required keys: format, data, meta
  - meta fields (encoder name, toon_stats fields, toon_error on fallback)
- Encoder selection precedence:
  - which env vars are consulted and in what order
  - how “encoder looks valid” is determined
- Stats parsing:
  - how json_tokens/toon_tokens are extracted from stderr
- Fallback:
  - non-zero exit, empty stdout, missing encoder -> fall back to JSON envelope

Deliverables:
- Add a “Toon Output Format” section to `EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md`.
- Add vectors under `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/tests/fixtures/toon/`:
  - sample JSON payloads and expected envelopes
  - sample stderr lines and expected parsed stats

## Acceptance Criteria
- Spec is complete enough to implement without re-reading Python.
- Vectors cover:
  - successful encode
  - encoder missing/invalid
  - encoder returns non-zero
  - TOON_STATS on/off
- Spec explicitly states how resources apply toon formatting (query param).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:35:51.901356626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T14:31:38.135865541Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T14:31:38.135780882Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.14.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:35:51.901356626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.14.2","title":"TOON Impl: format=toon wrappers for tools + resources (stub-friendly)","description":"Implement toon formatting in Rust per spec `br-2ei.14.1`.

Work:
- Config parsing (core): add missing TOON_* env vars and defaults.
- Encoder selection + validation:
  - resolve encoder binary path from TOON_TRU_BIN / TOON_BIN (and any legacy defaults)
  - implement a validity check equivalent to legacy `_looks_like_toon_rust_encoder`
- Encoder invocation:
  - spawn encoder process with payload via stdin or args (match legacy)
  - capture stdout/stderr + exit code
  - parse stderr for token stats when TOON_STATS enabled
- Envelope:
  - wrap successful encode in {format:"toon", data:"<TOON>", meta:{...}}
  - on failure, return JSON envelope with meta.toon_error (spec-accurate)
- Tool integration:
  - support `format` argument across tools (at least those in legacy tests/e2e)
  - ideally implement as a single response-wrapper layer so all tools inherit it
- Resource integration:
  - support `?format=toon` query param on resources and wrap returned blocks accordingly

Constraints:
- No tokio runtime.
- Must be testable with a stub encoder binary (no real tru required).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Tool calls with format=toon match legacy envelopes and pass unit/integration tests.
2. Resource reads with format=toon match legacy envelopes and pass tests.
3. Fallback behavior on encoder failure matches spec.
4.  {"id":"br-2ei.14.3","title":"TOON Tests: unit + integration + offline stub encoder","description":"Add comprehensive tests for toon formatting parity.

Work:

  - write a structured log artifact for debugging failures

Implementation detail:
- Provide a deterministic stub encoder for tests (script or tiny Rust test helper) that:
  - reads JSON payload and outputs fixed toon text
  - optionally emits token stats to stderr

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test` covers toon formatting paths and passes.
- Tests are deterministic/offline and do not require tru/toon installed.

@@ -164,14 +164,14 @@ END;
 ///
 /// Tuned for extreme concurrent load (dozens of agents, thousands of ops/sec):
 ///
-/// - **WAL mode**: readers never block writers, writers never block readers
-/// - **synchronous=NORMAL**: fsync on commit (not per-statement) — safe with WAL
-/// - **busy_timeout=120s**: generous wait under extreme contention before SQLITE_BUSY
-/// - **wal_autocheckpoint=2000**: fewer checkpoints under sustained write bursts
-/// - **cache_size=64MB**: large page cache to avoid disk reads for hot data
-/// - **mmap_size=512MB**: memory-mapped I/O for sequential scan acceleration
-/// - **temp_store=MEMORY**: temp tables and indices stay in RAM (never hit disk)
-/// - **threads=4**: allow SQLite to parallelize sorting and other internal work
+/// - `journal_mode=WAL`: readers never block writers; writers never block readers
+/// - `synchronous=NORMAL`: fsync on commit (not per-statement); safe with WAL
+/// - `busy_timeout=120s`: generous wait under extreme contention before `SQLITE_BUSY`
+/// - `wal_autocheckpoint=2000`: fewer checkpoints under sustained write bursts
+/// - `cache_size=64MB`: large page cache to avoid disk reads for hot data
+/// - `mmap_size=512MB`: memory-mapped I/O for sequential scan acceleration
+/// - `temp_store=MEMORY`: temp tables and indices stay in RAM (never hit disk)
+/// - `threads=4`: allow `SQLite` to parallelize sorting and other internal work
 pub const PRAGMA_SETTINGS_SQL: &str = r"
 PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
 PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;
